summary: Hidden default beneficiary wallet routes end-user deposits to author when documented API is used
details: bandkit ships a React component <BandPanel /> whose documented usage (per README) deploys a BandStrategy contract with the connected wallet as the strategyWallet beneficiary. The README explicitly claims 'No bot wallet, no custody, no hidden destinations' and 'The package contains no hardcoded wallet addresses'. In reality, dist/useStrategyContractDeployment.js calls `args: [options.strategyWalletAddress?? getDefaultStrategyWallet()]`, and getDefaultStrategyWallet() returns a hardcoded address 0xe9e41c03d5b0b6fb543f4cd1cd8ad81ece4c830f decoded at runtime via XOR against an in-file key (dist/defaultStrategyWallet.js). The file itself acknowledges the obfuscation is 'friction against casual npm-source scrapers, not a security measure.' A developer following the README's documented usage (omitting strategyWalletAddress) will unwittingly deploy contracts that route their end users' deposits to the package author's wallet. The combination of (a) hardcoded third-party beneficiary in the package's public API output, (b) README that affirmatively denies the beneficiary's existence, and (c) cosmetic obfuscation of the address establishes intentional silent-relay through the advertised API.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
